opp.ais_why:
• Per the Apr 27 2026 rep note from Guillaume Pasquet: 'No update. KIV with BD.' Per Apr 13: 'Under internal reorg. FEO will clarify in May if project finally happen this FY.' Per Dec 08 2025: 'Slow process. Meeting on Dec 17 to review Bus case/Functional clarifications/Next steps. Partnering with IBM (only Zycus focus + strong exec connect).' Engagement is held up by internal reorg with no named-buyer AI conversation.
• Per the 9 Sep 2025 rep note: 'Demo well received. Request for ballpark to include into budget for FY26. Next step should be functional workshop. Competitive against Ariba & Ivalua. Business case is main criteria of decision.' Karen Zhang is the documented stakeholder but framed as cost/business-case driven, never AI. Product scope is just iSource;iSupplier - thinnest AI footprint in batch.
• Medium confidence: 2 valid Avoma meetings (fareast.com domain confirmed) - both completed but notes not pulled (timed out). Product scope deliberately excludes Merlin/Agentic/ANA. NextStep spans 18+ entries Apr 2025-Apr 2026 with consistent traditional procurement/business case language, never AI. AI Resistant by absence.

items.quote: Next_Step__c: None -> None across all field-history entries

metrics.narrative: The buyer has articulated strong qualitative pain but has not validated any ROI numbers. CFO Kah Sek Koh stated 06 May 2024 that 'procurement is in a mess today' and the team is 'running >3000 Tenders' annually (Next Step 06 May 2024). On the 17 Dec 2025 call Zycus and IBM presented a full modelled business case (sourcing cycle 45 to 30 days, contract cycle 50 to 30 days, supplier onboarding 40 to 22 days, PR-to-PO 10 to 3 days, ~40-60% time reduction), but every figure was a Zycus/IBM benchmark, not a buyer-confirmed baseline. The buyer requested a ballpark quote 09 Sep 2025 'to include into budget for FY26' and Karen requested revised pricing 26 Jun 2026 for the budget process, yet no buyer-owned savings, addressable-spend, or cycle-time baseline exists. Quantifying value with FEO's own finance data is the move; without it the business case cannot clear budget review.

sources: Contact Role: Chung Ming Yew, PMO, Decision Maker (Primary)
champion.narrative: No champion exists. Karen Zhang (Application Solution Manager, Evaluator) is the most engaged contact and the day-to-day project lead, but she is reactive: her last touch (26 Jun 2026) was a passive request for revised pricing for the budget file, and she stated the project depends entirely on budget approval she does not control. She has not introduced Zycus to the CFO, driven the business case, or pushed back on the incumbent. Chung Ming Yew (Senior Manager, PMO, primary Decision Maker) and Chester Chung Ming YEW (Procurement Manager) built strong rapport in 2024 but have gone quiet since. Jenny Ku (budget owner) is passive. What is missing: an internal advocate with CFO access who will drive budget approval and own the business case. Developing that champion outranks any commercial step.

competition.narrative: Zycus competes against SAP Ariba (incumbent) and Ivalua, with GEP and Coupa having appeared on the original 2024 longlist and since faded. SAP is deeply entrenched: the buyer runs PR-to-PO-to-payment and invoicing in SAP and explicitly will not move that downstream layer, and they renewed SAP for 2025 (Next Step 20 Aug 2024). The wedge is upstream: FEO is 'not happy with Ariba' (Next Step 03 Mar 2025) and lacks catalog/guided buying and modern upstream sourcing, and the Zycus Gen AI value prop resonated at the IBM Exec Breakfast 26 Feb 2025. The path to win is to scope Zycus as upstream iSource/iSupplier with clean SAP hand-off, neutralizing incumbent inertia. No new competitive evidence surfaced this run, so the field ranking is carried forward.

sources: Avoma call 17 Dec 2025 (catalog gap, indirect/tail spend)
identify_pain.narrative: The pain is well established and owned by procurement and finance. The CFO stated procurement 'is in a mess today' and the team runs '>3000 Tenders' annually with no upstream sourcing system (Next Step 06 May 2024). On the 17 Dec 2025 call the buyer confirmed they lack catalog/guided purchasing ('catalog purchasing is something that we want to look at as part of this... Do you have that today? No'), driving manual effort on the ~30% indirect/tail spend that SAP does not handle well, plus maverick spend. The confirmed pain is upstream sourcing and supplier onboarding inefficiency and the absence of catalog buying; the inferred pain is spend-visibility and savings leakage on indirect categories. Pain owner is the procurement function (Jenny Ku / Chung Ming Yew) with the CFO as sponsor.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are genuinely unknown because the deal has never reached commercials. No MSA/SOW, legal, InfoSec, or procurement-channel discussion has occurred. The only paper-process signal is that any solution must integrate into FEO's SAP ERP and their existing OpenText invoicing stack, and that IBM is the implementation partner (Next Step 08 Dec 2025). Current contract expiration on the incumbent is not documented. Visibility would come from asking Jenny Ku's procurement team for the formal budget-approval and vendor-selection steps and the SAP integration/InfoSec review gates for FY2027.

sources: Contact Role: Kah Sek Koh, CFO, Executive Sponsor
Task 15 Jun 2026 Horizon invite to Kah Sek Koh
economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is identified but not engaged. Kah Sek Koh (CFO and Executive Director of Finance) is the Executive Sponsor on the Salesforce contact roles and is the budget authority, and Next Step 06 May 2024 shows the CFO personally flagged the project as urgent. However, the CFO has never appeared on a call and did not respond to the 15 Jun 2026 Horizon event invitation. Budget sits with Jenny Ku (Head of Purchasing & Contracts) whose team received the business case Oct 2025 without responding. No Zycus contact has secured CFO access, and commercials/pricing have never been discussed with the budget owner. Opening a path to Kah Sek Koh (via Chung Ming Yew or finance sponsor the relevant stakeholder) is the gating move.

decision_process.narrative: The process the buyer is actually running is a slow, IBM-partnered, business-case-first evaluation that is currently frozen by an internal reorg. IBM originated the opportunity (2023) and co-presents (Gloria of IBM led case studies on the 17 Dec 2025 call), and Zycus is IBM's focus vendor with 'strong exec connect' (Next Step 08 Dec 2025). The documented process is: build business case, secure FY budget approval through procurement (Jenny Ku) and finance, then resume evaluation. Approval is stalled: eval will not resume until Nov/Dec 2026 per Karen 26 Jun 2026. The stage even regressed from the relevant stakeholder back to Qualified on 24 Apr 2026, confirming the process has not advanced. A written, buyer-owned timeline for the budget cycle is missing and would create visibility.

sources: Avoma call 17 Dec 2025 (SAP integration, dual approval matrix, catalog purchasing)
decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's primary decision criterion is business-case strength: Next Step 09 Sep 2025 states 'Business case is main criteria of decision.' The 17 Dec 2025 call surfaced the concrete technical criteria that matter to FEO: clean integration with their SAP backbone (they run PR-to-PO-to-payment and 3-way-match/invoicing in SAP via an OpenText/Kofax model and will NOT move that out), avoidance of maintaining dual approval matrices, and catalog/guided buying which they do not have today. Karen's IT stakeholder said 'to take this out of SAP might have some issues' and pushed the model where Zycus generates the PR/PO for indirect/tail spend and hands off to the relevant stakeholder is confirmed as upstream (iSource/iSupplier) with SAP retained downstream.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — SAP Ariba is the strongest current threat as the entrenched incumbent: FEO runs downstream P2P and invoicing in SAP and will not replace it, and renewed SAP for 2025 (Next Step 20 Aug 2024). The opening is upstream, where the buyer is 'not happy with Ariba' (03 Mar 2025) and has no catalog/guided buying. Ivalua is the live named alternative from the 09 Sep 2025 demo read ('competitive against Ariba & Ivalua'). GEP and Coupa were on the original 2024 RFP longlist but have faded with no mention since. No new competitive evidence this run, so ranking is carried forward, not re-scored.
bullets.text: A competitor is pushing back — SAP Ariba is the strongest current threat as the entrenched incumbent: FEO runs downstream P2P and invoicing in SAP and will not replace it, and renewed SAP…

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS_Score 2.0, AIS_Status Negative. Calls confirm the read: the buyer frames the project around cost, cycle time, and SAP integration, never AI. Merlin was demoed once (17 Dec 2025) but the scope excludes agentic modules. AI is not a decision driver here.

Chung Ming Yew and Chester Chung Ming YEW had strong 2024 rapport but have gone silent, and budget owner Jenny Ku is passive. No one is driving budget approval, owning the business case, or navigating the reorg.

The Merlin intake demo shown on 17 Dec 2025 and the Gen AI value prop that resonated at the IBM Exec Breakfast (26 Feb 2025) are latent interest, but the product scope (iSource;iSupplier) deliberately excludes Merlin/Agentic/ANA, so the agentic story is not part of the deal.

On the 17 Dec 2025 call Zycus and IBM presented a full modelled business case (sourcing cycle 45 to 30 days, contract cycle 50 to 30 days, supplier onboarding 40 to 22 days, PR-to-PO 10 to 3 days, ~40-60% time reduction), but every figure was a Zycus/IBM benchmark, not a buyer-confirmed baseline. The buyer requested a ballpark quote 09 Sep 2025 'to include into budget for FY26' and Karen requested revised pricing 26 Jun 2026 for the budget process, yet no buyer-owned savings, addressable-spend, or cycle-time baseline exists. Quantifying value with FEO's own finance data is the move; without it the business case cannot clear budget review.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: FAR EAST ORGANISATION PTE. LTD. — Qualified · $350K · closes 18 Mar

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 11 is carried by momentum 55 vs stage-expected 50; exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (moving forward): Next Step carries 8 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (11), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 8 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 2.0, AIS_Status Negative. Calls confirm the read: the buyer frames the project around cost, cycle time, and SAP integration, never AI. Merlin was demoed once (17 Dec 2025) but the scope excludes agentic modules. AI is not a decision driver here.

items.change: Karen Zhang confirmed eval resumes Nov/Dec 2026 if budget approved, award mid-2027; requested revised pricing for the budget cycle (rep added 20% uplift).
Deal owner changed from Guillaume Pasquet to Carl Kimball, resetting the account relationship.
Stage regressed from Formal Evaluation back to Qualified.
IBM and Zycus delivered a full business-case and demo session (cycle-time savings model, Merlin intake demo); no competitor present, buyer focused on SAP integration and catalog buying.

deal_movement.summary: The deal is cold and frozen. The only recent motion is rep-side: a new owner (Carl Kimball, 06 Jun 2026) and a revised quote for the buyer's budget file (26 Jun 2026). The buyer confirmed evaluation will not resume until Nov/Dec 2026, and the stage regressed from Formal Evaluation to Qualified on 24 Apr 2026.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cold and rep-driven. The last buyer call was 6.5 months ago, the newest touches are Zycus outreach and event invites (mostly declined), and the buyer has explicitly deferred the project to a late-2026 budget decision.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 258 (close 2027-03-18, today 2026-07-03). Opp created 20 Jun 2023, 1,109 days old; entered Qualified stage repeatedly, most recently 24 Apr 2026 (moved Formal Evaluation to Qualified, a stage REGRESSION). Budget approval was expected end Oct 2025 per Next Step 26 Sep 2025; it is now 26 Jun 2026, 8 months overdue. Last buyer call 17 Dec 2025 (6.5 months ago). Last Salesforce activity 26 Jun 2026 is rep-initiated pricing exchange (Karen asked for a revised quote, Carl added a 20% uplift), NOT buyer engagement. Close date has slipped repeatedly: Q3 2025 (Aug 2024) to 2025-12-22 to 2026-06-26 to 2026-09-18 to 2027-03-18. Buyer now states eval resumes Nov/Dec 2026 and award mid-2027, implying a further 9-12 month slip. The 2027-03-18 date is not credible: the buyer will not restart evaluation for ~5 months.
evidence: Budget approval 8+ months overdue. Business case submitted to Jenny Ku's procurement team Oct 2025 per Next Step 27 Oct 2025; no approval as of 26 Jun 2026.

Karen Zhang stated 26 Jun 2026 'project eval to resume in Nov/Dec if budgets get approved. Project likely to award next year (Jun/Jul)' per Next Step 26 Jun 2026 and her Clari email 26 Jun 2026: 'implementation will be planned for 2027 if the budget is approved... evaluation phase likely to begin towards the end of this year or early next year.'

Internal reorg has frozen decision-making Mar-Jun 2026. Next Step 13 Apr 2026: 'Under internal reorg. FEO will clarify in May if project finally happen this FY.' That clarification never came. Next Step 27 Apr 2026: 'No update. KIV with BD.'

No two-way buyer engagement in 6.5 months. Last Avoma call 17 Dec 2025 (IBM/Zycus business-case + demo session). Owner changed from Guillaume Pasquet to Carl Kimball 06 Jun 2026, resetting the relationship.

Economic Buyer CFO Kah Sek Koh has never engaged on a call. CFO flagged urgency 06 May 2024 per Next Step ('CFO has raised this project as urgent as procurement is in a mess today') but no CFO participation since; a 15 Jun 2026 Horizon invite to Kah Sek Koh drew no response.
Forecast category Pipeline with no champion, no EB engagement, no buyer call in 6.5 months, budget 8 months overdue, and a repeated stage regression is indefensible.
north_star_verdict.headline: Budget approval 8+ months overdue, internal reorg has frozen decision-making, no buyer call in 6.5 months, and the CFO economic buyer has been disengaged for 25+ months. Karen Zhang stated 26 Jun 2026 that project evaluation will not resume until Nov/Dec 2026 IF budgets are approved, with award expected mid-2027. This is structural budget risk, not a near-term slip.

flags: Secure one accountable buyer sponsor with CFO access before more presales; the deal has run 3 years with no champion and no economic-buyer engagement.
Business case is the buyer's stated decision criterion but remains Zycus/IBM-built; get finance (Esther Chua) or Karen to co-own the ROI model with FEO's own spend data so it survives budget review.
Neutralize SAP incumbent inertia: scope the win to upstream iSource/iSupplier with a proven clean SAP hand-off and no dual approval matrices, the exact concern IT raised on 17 Dec 2025.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a 3-year-old, frozen, budget-blocked deal with no champion and no EB access. The win strategy is to re-establish an accountable buyer sponsor, make the business case buyer-owned, and de-risk SAP integration.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the strongest current threat as the entrenched incumbent: FEO runs downstream P2P and invoicing in SAP and will not replace it, and renewed SAP for 2025 (Next Step 20 Aug 2024). The opening is upstream, where the buyer is 'not happy with Ariba' (03 Mar 2025) and has no catalog/guided buying. Ivalua is the live named alternative from the 09 Sep 2025 demo read ('competitive against Ariba & Ivalua'). GEP and Coupa were on the original 2024 RFP longlist but have faded with no mention since. No new competitive evidence this run, so ranking is carried forward, not re-scored.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is not landing. AIS is 2.0 (Negative). The buyer is cost and business-case driven; every conversation is about cycle-time savings and SAP integration, not AI. The Merlin intake demo shown on 17 Dec 2025 and the Gen AI value prop that resonated at the IBM Exec Breakfast (26 Feb 2025) are latent interest, but the product scope (iSource;iSupplier) deliberately excludes Merlin/Agentic/ANA, so the agentic story is not part of the deal.

items.quote: Business case is main criteria of decision
items.source: Next Step 09 Sep 2025; Avoma call 17 Dec 2025
items.criterion: Quantified business case (stated main decision criterion)

items.quote: to take this out of SAP might have some issues... we need to maintain two different approval matrixes
items.criterion: Clean integration with SAP ERP and existing OpenText invoicing (no dual approval matrices)

items.quote: catalog purchasing is something that we want to look at as part of this... Do you have that today? No
items.criterion: Catalog and guided (Merlin) buying for indirect/tail spend

items.quote: procurement is in a mess today... running >3000 Tenders
items.criterion: Upstream sourcing and supplier onboarding efficiency

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is strong on the upstream sourcing/supplier problem and on catalog buying, but exposed on the buyer's stated top criterion (a quantified, buyer-owned business case) and dependent on proving clean SAP integration without dual approval matrices.

hard.ais_why:
• Per the Apr 27 2026 rep note from Guillaume Pasquet: 'No update. KIV with BD.' Per Apr 13: 'Under internal reorg. FEO will clarify in May if project finally happen this FY.' Per Dec 08 2025: 'Slow process. Meeting on Dec 17 to review Bus case/Functional clarifications/Next steps. Partnering with IBM (only Zycus focus + strong exec connect).' Engagement is held up by internal reorg with no named-buyer AI conversation.
• Per the 9 Sep 2025 rep note: 'Demo well received. Request for ballpark to include into budget for FY26. Next step should be functional workshop. Competitive against Ariba & Ivalua. Business case is main criteria of decision.' Karen Zhang is the documented stakeholder but framed as cost/business-case driven, never AI. Product scope is just iSource;iSupplier - thinnest AI footprint in batch.
• Medium confidence: 2 valid Avoma meetings (fareast.com domain confirmed) - both completed but notes not pulled (timed out). Product scope deliberately excludes Merlin/Agentic/ANA. NextStep spans 18+ entries Apr 2025-Apr 2026 with consistent traditional procurement/business case language, never AI. AI Resistant by absence.
